tracks.name: All Camp Activities
tracks.banner: Some programs require advance sign-up and may have limited spots including Archery and Sonora Taqueria! These programs are noted on the schedule, so sign-up early and don't miss out!

events.title: Welcome Campers!
events.description: Come check-in and get your welcome packet at the Volleyball Court. Late arrivals can check in at the store during extended hours.

events.title: New Camper Tour
events.description: Meet at the volleyball court for a tour of camp. All ages welcome.

events.title: Manager's Welcome Mixer & Kids' Prison Ball
events.description: Drop off the kids at the volleyball court for some prison ball and head to the Lair Lodge for the **Manager's Welcome Mixer** with your Oski Manager, Jonathan, and your Program Director, Ellie. We'll bring the kids over to dinner.

events.title: Welcome Campfire!
events.description: Meet your 2026 Camp Oski Staff at the Stage for some laughs and information about your week at our introductory show.

events.title: Creekside Readings & Music
events.description: Come join the Music Director, Izzy, for relaxing music and inspirational readings. Stroll to a quiet grove just below D Circle by the creek. It's a great way to start the week. Camper participation is encouraged.

events.title: Wacky Sunday Pool Show!
events.description: Bring the whole family to the pool for some silliness, pool games, and root beer floats.

events.title: Inner Tube Water Polo Match
events.description: Campers vs. Staffers at the pool. C'mon campers, show off your athleticism. 18 and over please.

events.title: Guest Speaker: Thomas M. Philip
events.description: Guest Speaker **Thomas M. Philip**, Professor of Education at UC Berkeley, will speak on “Technology, Learning, and Justice.” Come think about how technology is reshaping learning and its implications for schools and society, especially issues of educational equity and racial justice. Bring a flashlight or headlamp to illuminate your puzzling! Meet near the Gaga Pit by the bridge at 8pm to walk to the Gold Stage by 8:15.

events.title: Sonora Taqueria Pop Up at the Vista Lounge
events.description: Join us for an adults-only street taco night at the Vista Lodge! Complete with beer, wine, and margaritas. Sign up and pay in advance at the store. **Spots are limited.**

events.title: Family Disco Bingo in the Dining Hall
events.description: Wear disco clothes or any silly clothes, and bring 50 cents for a card. Oh, and be prepared to boogie down!!

events.title: Star Party with UC Berkeley Astronomy Professor Alex Filippenko!
events.description: Join Alex after Disco Bingo, and enjoy some incredible night sky viewing with his high-powered telescopes. Dress warmly.

events.title: Preparing for College
events.description: For teens and parents of teens, come to the Teen Lodge and hear personal experiences from our veteran staffers and learn how they got into college. The Teen Lodge is located between Camps Gold and Blue.

events.title: Walk Together to Lunch at the Lake!
events.description: Meet at the Gaga Pit to walk over to Lunch at the Lake.

events.title: Vista Lounge Prime Rib Dinner
events.description: Join us for a gourmet prime rib dinner with all the trimmings at Vista Lodge. Arrive at 6:00 PM for wine and appetizers; dinner is served at 6:30 PM. Sign up and pay in advance at the store. **Seating is limited.**

events.title: Guest Speaker: Alex Filippenko
events.description: Guest Speaker **Alex Filippenko**, voted by Cal students as the "best professor" a record 9 times, presents "The North Pole Marathon and Sights from the Arctic." Last July, Alex was the principal speaker on a cruise to the geographic North Pole, where he completed the North Pole Marathon (running 26.2 miles on the frozen ocean). He will share photographs and stories about the midnight Sun, atmospheric phenomena, stunning landscapes and seascapes, and numerous kinds of animals including polar bears and blue whales. Meet near the Gaga Pit by the bridge at 8pm to walk to the Gold Stage by 8:15.

events.title: Family Sing-along!
events.description: B.Y.O. voice and instrument to the Stage for a fun evening.

events.title: Vista Lounge Dinner!
events.description: Join us for an adults-only, gourmet prime rib dinner with all the trimmings at Vista Lodge. Arrive at 6:00 PM for wine and appetizers; dinner is served at 6:30 PM. Sign up and pay in advance at the store. **Seating is limited.**

events.title: Oski Revue!
events.description: Join the Music Director, Izzy, at the Stage for our camper talent show!

events.title: Stargazing at Camp Gold
events.description: Stargazing at the Mike Koll Softball Field at Camp Gold with your Nature Directors. Dress warmly!

events.title: ADULT DANCE at Vista Lodge!
events.description: Mix, mingle and boogie with your fellow campers from Blue, Gold, and Oski. Beer and wine provided -- **21+ only.**

events.title: Pinecone Contest
events.description: Pinecone Contest at the Oski Stage, right before the Hoot!

events.title: The Legendary Hootenanny
events.description: The Legendary Hootenanny at the Oski Stage! Yeehaww!

events.title: Adult Camper vs. Staff Softball
events.description: Adult Camper vs. Staff Softball at the Mike Koll Softball Field at Camp Gold, including a break for the Oski kids' inning. Some gloves available. Beverages provided.

events.title: Athletic Awards and Tie Dye Runway Show
events.description: Athletic Awards and Tie Dye Runway Show - at the stage right before the Friday Night Show

events.title: The World Famous Friday Night Campfire Show
events.description: The World Famous Friday Night Campfire Show at the Stage!

events.title: Check Out
events.description: We'll be closing our gate at 10:00 AM to prepare for next week's campers. Drive carefully and we'll see you next year! Don't forget to post and share your photos using #lairofthegoldenbear

tracks.name: Nature/Hiking
tracks.banner: *Sign-ups for hikes will be available at the Store. Sign-ups are required for all hiking programs and spots are limited* Please be prepared to drive for hiking programs.

events.title: Introductory Hiking Meeting
events.description:
Introductory hiking meeting with your Nature and Hiking team! Meet at the volleyball courts to discuss the hikes for the week, and to sign-up.

**Sign-ups for hikes will be available at the Store. Sign-ups are required for all hiking programs and spots are limited.**

*Please be prepared to drive for hiking programs.*

events.title: Sunset Wine and Cheese "Hike"
events.description:
Sunset Wine and Cheese "Hike" (ages 21+). It's not truly a hike. More of a short stroll, but the view is gorgeous! Wear warm clothes, bring a cup, and meet at the Volleyball court with your car.

**Sign-ups for hikes will be available at the Store. Sign-ups are required for all hiking programs and spots are limited.**

*Please be prepared to drive for hiking programs.*

events.title: All Day Hike!
events.description:
Pack a lunch and bring water and sunscreen. Meet at the volleyball court with your car if you can drive.

**Sign-ups for hikes will be available at the Store. Sign-ups are required for all hiking programs and spots are limited.**

*Please be prepared to drive for hiking programs.*

events.title: Stand Up Paddle Boarding at Pinecrest Lake
events.location: Pinecrest Lake (far-right entrance near fishing dock)
events.description: Sessions are one hour each. Participants must be at least 12 years old, and minors must be accompanied by an adult. Swim experience required. To participate, campers must book and pay at the store ($25), sign a waiver, and receive a slip with their time slot and directions. Bring this slip with you to the lake. The meeting spot is located at the far-right entrance to the lake, near the fishing dock. See you there!
